Note 4 — Financial Instruments

Non-marketable equity securities

Our non-marketable equity securities are investments in privately held companies without readily determinable market values. The carrying value of our non-marketable equity securities is adjusted to fair value upon observable transactions for identical or similar investments of the same issuer or impairment.

As of September 30, 2024, the carrying value of our non-marketable equity securities was $33.7 billion. Non-marketable equity securities cumulative net gain (loss) is comprised of $35.4 billion of gains and $2.5 billion of losses (including impairment) as of the same date.

We use option pricing models, the market comparable approach, and the common stock equivalent method to estimate fair value for non-marketable equity securities. The increase in carrying value during the third quarter of 2024 primarily reflects observable transactions involving our investment in non-marketable equity securities, primarily one investee.
